level_signals: Role operates at platform leadership level, not feature PM
Cross-organizational scope, executive communication, startup-founder autonomy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of Product Management experience, with significant experience building developer platforms, CI/CD systems, testing/quality tooling, or internal engineering products.
Demonstrated success shipping LLM-powered or agent-driven products into production.
Deep familiarity with agentic patterns (tool use, planning/execution loops, structured prompting, evaluation strategies).
Experience designing platform-scale products with APIs, extensibility, governance, and enterprise adoption in mind.
Strong architectural intuition and the ability to read and reason about code.
Comfort collaborating directly with senior engineers on implementation trade-offs.
Experience defining telemetry requirements and AI evaluation metrics.
Exceptional discovery skills: problem framing, hypothesis formation, prioritization, ROI analysis.
Experience defining measurable success criteria and evaluation frameworks for AI systems.
Ability to balance long-term platform investments with near-term demonstrable wins.
Proven track record driving cross-team initiatives involving engineering, data science, infrastructure, security, and compliance.
Ability to align diverse stakeholders around a bold but grounded vision.
Strong storytelling and executive communication skills

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: AI-powered SDLC platform spanning product discovery, design, prototyping, coding, testing, release, operations, and continuous improvement
scope.business_model: internal platform / developer experience, B2B fintech (national bank)
primary_metrics_inferred: AI-driven workflow reliability and safety scores
Cross-organizational adoption of AI-enabled development standards
Measurable impact of AI across SDLC stages
core_responsibilities: Own the multi-year product strategy for AI across the Product and Software Development Lifecycle.
Define standards for 'Spec-to-Code-to-Deploy' workflows, where structured specifications can trigger AI agents to generate production-ready code, tests, and documentation.
Define how AI agents integrate into specs, repositories, CI/CD pipelines, testing frameworks, and operational systems and the technologies required to enable it.
Run fast build-measure-learn loops with internal developer users.
Establish measurable evaluation frameworks to ensure AI-driven workflows are reliable, safe, and continuously improving.
Partner with Security, Legal, Architecture, and Infrastructure teams to define governance boundaries for AI autonomy.
Drive cross-organizational adoption of AI-enabled development standards.
Communicate clearly to senior leadership on strategy, trade-offs, and measurable impact.

green_flags: Clear and ambitious product scope — not a vague 'AI strategy' role
Startup-founder autonomy inside a large company (national bank)
Principal-level IC seniority signals significant trust and ownership
Explicit mention of partnering with principal engineers, not junior teams
Remote work appears available (no office mandate stated, Hawaii/Alaska exclusion implies broader remote eligibility)
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed — only stated it is 'listed below' but was not included in the JD text
Location type is ambiguous — no city or office requirement stated, but also no explicit remote confirmation
Buzzword density is high — 'AI in every facet', 'startup founder inside the company' etc. may indicate aspirational framing over concrete roadmap
No nice-to-haves stated — all requirements are framed as hard musts, leaving little flexibility signaled to candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Principal', 10+ years requirement, and organizational scope)
location.type (unclear — no city or office requirement named; Hawaii/Alaska exclusion implies remote-eligible but not confirmed)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from Hawaii/Alaska exclusion language, suggesting remote work is otherwise offered)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as national bank fintech with internal platform focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated explicitly; inferred from responsibilities)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD language 'Final base pay offer will be determined based on individual factors such as the candidate's experience, skills, and location')
ambiguities: Compensation range is referenced ('The base pay range for this role is listed below') but no actual numbers appear in the JD text as provided — treated as unknown.
Location type is not specified — the JD does not name a city or office, and does not explicitly say 'remote.' The Hawaii/Alaska exclusion implies remote eligibility but this is not confirmed.
No nice-to-have or preferred qualifications are listed; all requirements appear unconditional. This may mean the JD was written without soft requirements, or they were omitted.
